It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.

[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.

; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.

; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.

[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.

=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.

revision.text:
[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰}} * [[Gion Faith]]

'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰}} * [[Gion Faith]]

; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.

revision.text:
[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰}} * [[Gion Faith]]
== References == {{Reflist}}

However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.

; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.

[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.

However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.

'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.

[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.

The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.

[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.

[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:qq]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.

[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.

; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.

'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰}} * [[Gion Faith]]

=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰}} * [[Gion Faith]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
