[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.

The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}.== See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰}} * [[Gion Faith]]

[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}. == See Also! == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰}} * [[Gion Faith]]

; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰}} * [[Gion Faith]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|牛頭天王" to "|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō"
revision.text:
[[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsushima Faith}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:11]] <!--Tsushima Faith-->[[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[Category:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]][[en:Tsushima Faith]] == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰}} * [[Gion Faith]]

However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰}} * [[Gion Faith]]

revision.text:
<!--Tsushima Faith--> == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰}} * [[Gion Faith]]
== References == {{Reflist}}

This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋|12=draft}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋|12=draft}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家|12=draft}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市|12=draft}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|12=draft}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市|12=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町|12=draft}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村|12=draft}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭|12=draft}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市|12=draft}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰|12=draft}} * [[Gion Faith]]

<!--Tsushima Faith--> == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市|12=draft}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|12=draft}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰|12=draft}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭|12=draft}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請|12=draft}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋|12=draft}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋|12=draft}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家|12=draft}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市|12=draft}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|12=draft}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市|12=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町|12=draft}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村|12=draft}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭|12=draft}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市|12=draft}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰|12=draft}} * [[Gion Faith]]

== Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|12=draft|12=draft}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|12=draft|12=draft}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭|12=draft|12=draft}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|12=draft|12=draft}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋|12=draft|12=draft}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋|12=draft|12=draft}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家|12=draft|12=draft}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|12=draft|12=draft}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市|12=draft|12=draft}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市|12=draft|12=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市|12=draft|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市|12=draft|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭|12=draft|12=draft}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市|12=draft|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市|12=draft|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[Gion Faith]]

=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Gion Faith]]

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:津島信仰 up to rev 100882321
revision.text:
'''津島信仰'''（つしましんこう）とは、[[愛知県]][[津島市]]にある[[津島神社]]を中心として[[東海地方]]を中心に分布する[[神道]]の信仰である。
[[スサノオ]]・[[牛頭天王]]に対する信仰であり、[[祇園信仰]]の一種ということになるが、津島信仰独特の信仰も見られ<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=松山由布子 |title=津島神社所蔵『牛頭天王講式』と奥三河伝来『牛頭天王五段式』の関わりについて |journal=愛知県史研究 |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=愛知県 |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>、[[尾張津島天王祭|津島祭]]がひとつのピークである<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=黒田一充 |title=津島信仰のお仮屋 |journal=関西大学博物館紀要 |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=関西大学博物館 |year=2009 |month=mar |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>。
== 概要 == [[名古屋市|名古屋]]ではスサノオ・牛頭天王は「お天王さま」と呼ばれた。「津島代参講」と呼ばれる[[講]]が各地に作られ、講の代表者が津島神社に代参して神札を受け、講の構成員に配布した。時代が下ると、町内に津島神社を[[勧請]]して小祠を作るようになり、さらには、各戸の屋上に牛頭天王を勧請して祀る「屋根神様」の信仰が生まれた。
スサノオ・牛頭天王の[[神紋]]は[[木瓜紋]]であり、また東海地方を拠点とし、津島神社を崇敬していた[[織田家]]は木瓜を[[家紋]]とし、この二つは容易に結び付けられた<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=赤木妙子 |title=織田信長の自己神格化と津嶋牛頭天王 |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=史学 |issn=03869334 |publisher=三田史学会 |year=1991 |month=apr |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>。
== 全国の津島神社 == 津島神社より勧請を受けた神社は、東海地方を中心に日本全国に約3千社ある。その多くはかつては「牛頭天王社」「天王社」を称していたが、明治の神仏分離の際、総本社の津島牛頭天王社（津島天王社）が津島神社に改称したことから、同名の「津島神社」に改称した。 === 総本社 === * [[津島神社]] - [[愛知県]][[津島市]]にある神社。'''津島信仰'''の総本社
=== 東北地方 === ; [[山形県]] * [[津嶋神社 (長井市)]] - [[山形県]][[長井市]]にある神社。 ; [[宮城県]] * [[津島神社 (登米市)]] - [[宮城県]][[登米市]]にある神社。 ; [[福島県]] * [[津島神社 (二本松市)]] - [[福島県]][[二本松市]]針道にある神社。 === 関東地方 === ; [[埼玉県]] * [[玉津島神社]] - [[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]に鎮座する神社。 ; [[東京都]] * [[津島神社 (渋谷区)]] - [[東京都]][[渋谷区]][[代々木]]に鎮座する神社。 * [[津島神社 (稲城市)]] - [[東京都]][[稲城市]]にある神社。[[大麻止乃豆乃天神社]]の[[境内社]]。 * [[津島神社 (西東京市)]] - [[東京都]][[西東京市]]田無にある神社。柳沢津島神社。 ; [[神奈川県]] * [[津島神社 (真鶴町)]] - [[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[真鶴町]]にある神社。 === 中部地方 === ; [[新潟県]] * [[津島神社 (長岡市)]] - [[新潟県]][[長岡市]]にある神社。 ; [[山梨県]] * [[津島神社 (韮崎市)]] - [[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (道志村)]] - [[山梨県]][[南都留郡]][[道志村]]上善之木にある神社。 ; [[長野県]] * [[津島神社 (岡谷市)]] - [[長野県]][[岡谷市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (上松町)]] - [[長野県]][[木曽郡]][[上松町]]小川にある神社 * [[津島神社 (宮田村)]] - [[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[宮田村]]にある神社。[[奇祭]][[宮田祇園祭]]（あばれ[[神輿]]）が行われる神社。 * [[津島神社 (箕輪町)]] - [[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[箕輪町]]中箕輪に鎮座する神社。 ; [[静岡県]] * [[津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)]] - [[静岡県]][[静岡市]][[駿河区]]馬渕にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (静岡市清水区)]] - 静岡県静岡市[[清水区]]興津清見寺町にある神社。 * [[八幡津島神社]] - 静岡県[[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (浜松市東区)]] - 静岡県[[浜松市]][[中央区 (浜松市)|中央区]]中野町にある神社。 ; [[岐阜県]] * [[津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)]] - 岐阜県[[岐阜市]]津島町にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (関市)]] - [[岐阜県]][[関市]][[洞戸]][[通元寺]]にある神社。毎年夏の提灯祭りが有名。 * [[津島神社 (各務原市)]] - [[岐阜県]][[各務原市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (可児市)]] - 岐阜県[[可児市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (海津市)]] - 岐阜県[[海津市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (瑞浪市)]] - 岐阜県[[瑞浪市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (下呂市金山町)]] - 岐阜県[[下呂市]]金山町大船渡にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (下呂市小坂町小坂町)]] - 岐阜県下呂市小坂町小坂町にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (下呂市小坂町門坂)]] - 岐阜県下呂市小坂町門坂にある神社。 ; [[愛知県]] * [[津島神社 (名古屋市西区)]] - [[名古屋市]][[西区 (名古屋市)|西区]][[則武新町]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (豊根村)]] - [[愛知県]][[北設楽郡]][[豊根村]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (春日井市)]] - 愛知県[[春日井市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (小牧市中央)]] - 愛知県[[小牧市]]中央にある神社。 * [[津嶋神社 (小牧市二重堀)]] - 愛知県[[小牧市]]二重堀にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (愛西市)]] - 愛知県[[愛西市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (北名古屋市)]] - 愛知県[[北名古屋市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (岩倉市)]] - 愛知県[[岩倉市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (清須市)]] - 愛知県[[清須市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (常滑市)]] - 愛知県[[常滑市]]にある神社。 * [[津島社 (豊山町)]] - 愛知県[[西春日井郡]][[豊山町]]にある神社。 * [[津島社 (設楽町)]] - 愛知県[[北設楽郡]][[設楽町]]にある神社。 === 近畿地方 === ; [[京都府]] * [[津島神社 (南山城村)]] - [[京都府]][[相楽郡]][[南山城村]]高尾にある神社。 ; [[大阪府]] * [[津嶋部神社]] - [[大阪府]][[守口市]]にある神社。 ; [[奈良県]] * [[津島神社 (田原本町)]] - [[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]にある神社。 ; [[和歌山県]] * [[玉津島神社]] - [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]に鎮座する神社。 === 四国地方 === ; [[香川県]] * [[津嶋神社 (三豊市)]] - [[香川県]][[三豊市]]にある神社。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[氷川信仰]]
== 外部リンク == * {{Cite journal|和書|author=松下孜 |title=天王信仰と津島御師の活動:御立符と檀那場をめぐって |journal=愛知県史研究 |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=愛知県総務部法務文書課県史編さん室 |year=2010 |volume=14 |pages=15-37 |naid=110007528485 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.14.0_15 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.14.0_15}}
{{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:つしましんこう}} [[Category:津島神社|!つしましんこう]] [[Category:祇園信仰]] [[Category:信仰]]

=== 東北地方 === ; [[山形県]] * [[津嶋神社 (長井市)]] - [[山形県]][[長井市]]にある神社。 ; [[宮城県]] * [[津島神社 (登米市)]] - [[宮城県]][[登米市]]にある神社。 ; [[福島県]] * [[津島神社 (二本松市)]] - [[福島県]][[二本松市]]針道にある神社。 === 関東地方 === ; [[埼玉県]] * [[玉津島神社]] - [[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]に鎮座する神社。 ; [[東京都]] * [[津島神社 (渋谷区)]] - [[東京都]][[渋谷区]][[代々木]]に鎮座する神社。 * [[津島神社 (稲城市)]] - [[東京都]][[稲城市]]にある神社。[[大麻止乃豆乃天神社]]の[[境内社]]。 * [[津島神社 (西東京市)]] - [[東京都]][[西東京市]]田無にある神社。柳沢津島神社。 ; [[神奈川県]] * [[津島神社 (真鶴町)]] - [[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[真鶴町]]にある神社。 === 中部地方 === ; [[新潟県]] * [[津島神社 (長岡市)]] - [[新潟県]][[長岡市]]にある神社。 ; [[山梨県]] * [[津島神社 (韮崎市)]] - [[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (道志村)]] - [[山梨県]][[南都留郡]][[道志村]]上善之木にある神社。 ; [[長野県]] * [[津島神社 (岡谷市)]] - [[長野県]][[岡谷市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (上松町)]] - [[長野県]][[木曽郡]][[上松町]]小川にある神社 * [[津島神社 (宮田村)]] - [[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[宮田村]]にある神社。[[奇祭]][[宮田祇園祭]]（あばれ[[神輿]]）が行われる神社。 * [[津島神社 (箕輪町)]] - [[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[箕輪町]]中箕輪に鎮座する神社。 ; [[静岡県]] * [[津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)]] - [[静岡県]][[静岡市]][[駿河区]]馬渕にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (静岡市清水区)]] - 静岡県静岡市[[清水区]]興津清見寺町にある神社。 * [[八幡津島神社]] - 静岡県[[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (浜松市東区)]] - 静岡県[[浜松市]][[中央区 (浜松市)|中央区]]中野町にある神社。 ; [[岐阜県]] * [[津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)]] - 岐阜県[[岐阜市]]津島町にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (関市)]] - [[岐阜県]][[関市]][[洞戸]][[通元寺]]にある神社。毎年夏の提灯祭りが有名。 * [[津島神社 (各務原市)]] - [[岐阜県]][[各務原市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (可児市)]] - 岐阜県[[可児市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (海津市)]] - 岐阜県[[海津市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (瑞浪市)]] - 岐阜県[[瑞浪市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (下呂市金山町)]] - 岐阜県[[下呂市]]金山町大船渡にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (下呂市小坂町小坂町)]] - 岐阜県下呂市小坂町小坂町にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (下呂市小坂町門坂)]] - 岐阜県下呂市小坂町門坂にある神社。 ; [[愛知県]] * [[津島神社 (名古屋市西区)]] - [[名古屋市]][[西区 (名古屋市)|西区]][[則武新町]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (豊根村)]] - [[愛知県]][[北設楽郡]][[豊根村]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (春日井市)]] - 愛知県[[春日井市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (小牧市中央)]] - 愛知県[[小牧市]]中央にある神社。 * [[津嶋神社 (小牧市二重堀)]] - 愛知県[[小牧市]]二重堀にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (愛西市)]] - 愛知県[[愛西市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (北名古屋市)]] - 愛知県[[北名古屋市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (岩倉市)]] - 愛知県[[岩倉市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (清須市)]] - 愛知県[[清須市]]にある神社。 * [[津島神社 (常滑市)]] - 愛知県[[常滑市]]にある神社。 * [[津島社 (豊山町)]] - 愛知県[[西春日井郡]][[豊山町]]にある神社。 * [[津島社 (設楽町)]] - 愛知県[[北設楽郡]][[設楽町]]にある神社。 === 近畿地方 === ; == Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Gion Faith]]

; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for its annual lantern festival.

== Tsushima Faith ==
'''Tsushima Faith''' (津島信仰, Tsushima Shinkō) is a Shinto belief system centered around {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is widely practiced mainly in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
It is a form of faith dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a branch of {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, Tsushima Faith also exhibits unique characteristics<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Yufuko Matsuyama |title=On the Relationship Between the 'Gozu Tennō Kōshiki' Owned by Tsushima Shrine and the 'Gozu Tennō Godan-shiki' Transmitted in Okumikawa |journal=Aichi Prefecture History Research |issn=1883-3799 |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |year=2017 |volume=21 |pages=57-72 |naid=130007696076 |doi=10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>, with the {{ill|Tenno Matsuri|ja|尾張津島天王祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} serving as a major event<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Kazumitsu Kuroda |title=The Temporary Shrine in Tsushima Faith |journal=Kansai University Museum Bulletin |issn=1341-4895 |publisher=Kansai University Museum |year=2009 |month=March |issue=15 |pages=1-17 |naid=110007334592 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10112/2887 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
In {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Susanoo and Gozu Tennō were known as "O-Tennō-sama" (お天王さま). Groups known as "Tsushima Daisan Kō" (津島代参講) were formed in various locations, and representatives from these groups would visit Tsushima Shrine to receive sacred talismans, which were then distributed to members. Over time, local communities began {{ill|enshrining|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tsushima Shrine within their towns, constructing small shrines. This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Gion Faith]]

This practice further evolved into the belief in "Rooftop Kami" (屋根神様), where individual households enshrined Gozu Tennō on their rooftops.
The {{ill|Kamon|ja|家紋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (family crest) associated with Susanoo and Gozu Tennō is the {{ill|Mokko|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (wood sorrel crest). The {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, based in the Tōkai region and devoted to Tsushima Shrine, also used the Mokko crest as their family emblem, reinforcing the connection between the two<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Taeko Akagi |title=Oda Nobunaga’s Self-Deification and Tsushima Gozu Tennō |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19910400-0127 |journal=Shigaku |issn=03869334 |publisher=Mita Historical Society |year=1991 |month=April |volume=60 |issue=1 |pages=127-140 |naid=110007410625 |accessdate=2021-07-01}}</ref>.
== Tsushima Shrines Across Japan ==
Approximately 3,000 shrines across Japan, primarily in the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, have received enshrinement from Tsushima Shrine. Many of these were originally named "Gozu Tennō Shrine" (牛頭天王社) or "Tennō Shrine" (天王社). However, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the Meiji era, the main shrine, Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine (津島天王社), was renamed Tsushima Shrine, prompting many affiliated shrines to adopt the same name.
=== Main Shrine === * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|ja|津島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the principal shrine of Tsushima Faith.
=== Tōhoku Region === ; {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagai)|ja|津嶋神社 (長井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagai, Yamagata|ja|長井市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Tome)|ja|津島神社 (登米市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|津島神社 (二本松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Kantō Region === ; {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tamagushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|ja|深谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shibuya)|ja|津島神社 (渋谷区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shibuya, Tokyo|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Inagi)|ja|津島神社 (稲城市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Inagi, Tokyo|ja|稲城市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmatonozu-no-Ten Shrine|ja|大麻止乃豆乃天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nishitokyo)|ja|津島神社 (西東京市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo, Tokyo|ja|西東京市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|津島神社 (真鶴町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Chūbu Region === ; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagaoka)|ja|津島神社 (長岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|ja|長岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Okaya)|ja|津島神社 (岡谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Miyada)|ja|津島神社 (宮田村)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Miyada, Nagano|ja|宮田村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for the {{ill|Miyada Gion Festival|ja|宮田祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, featuring a violent mikoshi procession.
; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|津島神社 (静岡市駿河区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Shizuoka, Shizuoka|ja|静岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Gifu)|ja|津島神社 (岐阜市津島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Gifu, Gifu|ja|岐阜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Seki)|ja|津島神社 (関市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for its annual lantern festival.
; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|津島神社 (名古屋市西区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Kiyosu)|ja|津島神社 (清須市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kiyosu, Aichi|ja|清須市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Hikawa Faith|ja|氷川信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Gion Faith]]
